hero.question: Will Illinois win the 2026 NCAA Tournament?
hero.prob_take: At 14%, the crowd has basically written off Illinois as a tournament winner, pricing in either a collapse this season or a first-round exit even if they make the dance. The real question is whether a Big Ten team with tournament experience and March upside deserves even lower odds, or whether 14% undershoots a squad that could sneak out as a 6-8 seed and get hot at the right time.

daily_take.headline: Illinois at 14% looks like free money if the Big Ten stays intact
daily_take.deck: Illinois dropped 20 basis points overnight on thin volume, signaling weak hands rotating out. But the market is pricing in a doomsday scenario for the program that hasn't materialized. With Terrence Shannon Jr. returning and a reconstituted roster, 14% undervalues their tournament odds relative to peer Big Ten teams in similar positions. Sharp money should fade this panic.

sidebar.headline: Iran odds crushed 50bps in 24 hours, but $30M in volume suggests real flow, not noise. Watch for escalation signals before that rate gets cheaper.
sidebar.label: US Forces Enter Iran: 5.5%

sidebar.headline: Arizona's 35.9% jumped hard on two points of movement and half the Illinois volume. Relative value test: is Arizona really worth 2.5x Illinois at a weaker program with worse recruiting?
sidebar.label: Arizona 2026 NCAA Champion: 35.9%

sidebar.headline: Trump removal at 6.5% on $6K volume is basically a ghost market. Ignore until real money shows up.
